PO_ACTION_HISTORY.description: PO_ACTION_HISTORY contains information about the approval and control history of your purchasing documents.  There is one record in this table for each approval or control action an employee takes on a purchase order, purchase agreement, release, or requisition. Each row includes references to the document itself, the employee who acted on the document, the date of the action, the type of action taken on the document, and a note each employee can leave when taking an action on the document.  . Oracle Purchasing uses this information to display history information about documents and to forward documents in the approval process to the appropriate employee.  .

PO_APPROVED_SUPPLIER_LIST.description: PO_APPROVED_SUPPLIER_LIST stores the relationship between an item or commodity; a supplier, distributor, or manufacturer; ship-to organizations; and approval/certification status.

PO_ASL_ATTRIBUTES.description: PO_ASL_ATTRIBUTES stores all information for the supplier/item/organization relationship defined in PO_APPROVED_SUPPLIER_LIST.   This information is maintained separately to allow each organization to define its own attributes, even if using a Global ASL entry for the supplier/item relationship and approval status.

PO_ASL_DOCUMENTS.description: PO_ASL_DOCUMENTS stores sourcing references to supply agreements, blanket agreements, and catalog quotations  associated with particular suppliers and items in PO_APPROVED_SUPPLIER_LIST.

PO_ASL_STATUSES.description: PO_ASL_STATUSES stores the user-defined approval/certification statuses.

PO_CONFIG_COMPONENTS.description: PO_CONFIG_COMPONENTS stores current information about components (options and models) associated with configurable line items.  Only documents associated with a Configure to Order Style are allowed to have configurable to order lines in their component details are stored in this table.

PO_CONFIG_COMPONENTS_ARCHIVE.description: PO_CONFIG_COMPONENTS_ARCHIVE stores past and current information about components (options and models) associated with configurable line items.  Only documents associated with a Configure to Order Style are allowed to have configurable to order lines in their component details are stored in this table. You can retrieve revisions of the purchase order line using this table.  The latest archived revision will have TO_CO_SEQ set to '999999'.  You need one row for each document configuration entry you archive.  Each row contains the same information as the PO_CONFIG_COMPONENTS table.  Oracle Purchasing uses this information to ensure you keep copies of your legal documents.  Oracle Purchasing does not currently read or retrieve information in this table.

PO_CONFIG_COMPONENTS_DRAFT.description: PO_CONFIG_COMPONENTS_DRAFT stores draft changes information about components (options and models) associated with configurable line items that have not yet been accepted by buyer.  Only documents associated with a Configure to Order Style are allowed to have configurable to order lines in their component details are stored in this table. When the draft changes are accepted, the record is moved to PO_CONFIG_COMPONENTS.  Changes from buyer are automatically accepted. PO_CONFIG_COMPONENTS_DRAFT contains all columns from PO_CONFIG_COMPONENTS.

PO_CO_TEMPLATES_B.description: Base Table for PO Change Order Templates

PO_DISTRIBUTIONS_ALL.description: PO_DISTRIBUTIONS_ALL contains accounting distribution information for a purchase order shipment line. You need one row for each distribution line you attach to a purchase order shipment. There are four types of documents using distributions in Oracle Purchasing: Standard Purchase Orders, Planned Purchase Orders, Planned Purchase Order Releases and Blanket Purchase Order Releases. Each row includes the destination type, requestor ID, quantity ordered and deliver-to location for the distribution. Oracle Purchasing uses this information to record accounting and requisition information for purchase orders and releases. PO_DISTRIBUTIONS_ALL is one of five tables storing purchase order and release information. Some columns in PO_DISTRIBUTIONS_ALL contain information only if certain conditions exist: If you autocreate this accounting distribution from a requisition, REQ_DISTRIBUTION_ID corresponds to the ID of the requisition distribution you copy on the purchase order. If you use a foreign currency on your purchase order, Oracle Purchasing stores currency conversion information in RATE and RATE_DATE. If you use encumbrance, GL_ENCUMBERED_DATE and GL_ENCUMBERED_PERIOD_NAME contain encumbrance information Oracle Purchasing uses to create journal entries in Oracle General Ledger. If you do not autocreate the purchase order from online requisitions, REQ_LINE_REFERENCE_NUM and REQ_HEADER_REFERENCE_NUM contain the requisition number and requisition line number of the corresponding paper requisition. These two columns are not foreign keys to another table. If the distribution corresponds to a blanket purchase order release, PO_RELEASE_ID identifies this release. If SOURCE_DISTRIBUTION_ID has a value, the distribution is part of a planned purchase order release. If you cancel the distribution, Oracle Purchasing automatically updates QUANTITY_CANCELLED or GL_CANCELLED_DATE. Oracle Purchasing also enters UNENCUMBERED_AMOUNT if you use encumbrance. You enter distribution information in the Distributions window, accessible through the  Purchase Orders and Releases windows.

PO_DISTRIBUTIONS_ARCHIVE_ALL.description: PO_DISTRIBUTIONS_ARCHIVE_ALL contains archived distribution information. Oracle Purchasing only archives distributions for the following documents: Standard purchase orders, Planned purchase orders, Planned purchase order releases and Blanket purchase order releases. When you modify a distribution, Oracle Purchasing will archive it after you (re)approve the purchase order or after you print it (this depends on the purchasing system options). You can retrieve revisions of the distributions using this table. The latest archived revision will have LATEST_EXTERNAL_FLAG set to 'Y'. You need one row for each distribution archive. Each row contains the same information as the PO_DISTRIBUTIONS_ALL table. Oracle Purchasing uses this information to ensure you keep copies of your legal documents.

PO_DISTRIBUTIONS_DRAFT_ALL.description: PO_DISTRIBUTIONS_DRAFT_ALL stores draft changes to PO distribution that have not been accepted by buyer. A record in this table contains the proposed values for the corresponding record in the transaction table PO_DISTRIBUTIONS_ALL. When the draft changes are accepted, the record is moved to PO_DISTRIBUTIONS_ALL. Changes from buyer are automatically accepted. PO_DISTRIBUTIONS_DRAFT_ALL contains all columns from PO_DISTRIBUTIONS_ALL, as well as the following additional columns: 1. DRAFT_ID: primary key of the table; foreign key to PO_DRAFTS; 2. CHANGE_ACCEPTED_FLAG: acceptance status of the draft record; 3. DELETE_FLAG: flag indicating whether the corresponding record in transaction table should be deleted when the draft change is accepted.

PO_DISTRIBUTIONS_INTERFACE.description: PO_DISTRIBUTIONS_INTERFACE is the interface table that holds  distribution information for the PDOI program to create standard  purchase order. The PDOI program validates your data, derives or defaults additional  information and writes an error message for every validation that  fails into the PO_INTERFACE_ERRORS table. Scope: public Product: PO Display Name: PO Distributions Interface Category: BUSINESS_ENTITY PO_STANDARD_PURCHASE_ORDER

Document style defines the procurement style across the different stages of the procurement process.  It is used to control the display of the document and document usages. Within Purchasing, users can  define a single document style setting for the following type of documents: Standard Purchase Order, Global Agreement and Global  Contract.

PO_HAZARD_CLASSES_B.description: PO_HAZARD_CLASSES_B contains information about the hazardous materials classes you use in your business. Oracle Purchasing lets you record hazard class information to let you comply with Section 19 of the International Safety Rating System. You need one row for each hazard class you use.  . Oracle Purchasing uses this information to let you provide hazard class information on your purchase orders, requisitions, RFQs, and quotations. . .

PO_HAZARD_CLASSES_TL.description: PO_HAZARD_CLASSES_TL contains translated information about the hazard classes.

PO_HEADERS_ALL.description: PO_HEADERS_ALL contains header information for your purchasing documents. You need one row for each document you create. There are six types of documents that use PO_HEADERS_ALL: RFQs, Quotations, Standard purchase orders, Planned purchase orders, Blanket purchase orders and Contracts. Each row contains buyer information, supplier information, brief notes, foreign currency information, terms and conditions information, and the status of the document. Oracle Purchasing uses this information to record information that is related to a complete document. PO_HEADER_ID is the unique system-generated primary key and is invisible to the user. SEGMENT1 is the system-assigned number you use to identify the document in forms and reports. Oracle Purchasing generates SEGMENT1 using the PO_UNIQUE_IDENTIFIER_CONT_ALL table if you choose to let Oracle Purchasing generate document numbers for you. SEGMENT1 is not unique for the entire table. Different document types can share the same numbers. You can uniquely identify a row in PO_HEADERS_ALL using PRC_BU_ID, SEGMENT1, and TYPE_LOOKUP_CODE, or using PO_HEADER_ID. If APPROVED_FLAG is 'Y', the purchase order is approved. If your document type is a blanket purchase order, contract purchase order, RFQ, or quotation, Oracle Purchasing uses START_DATE and END_DATE to store the valid date range for the document. Oracle Purchasing only uses BLANKET_TOTAL_AMOUNT for blanket purchase orders or contract purchase orders. If you autocreate a quotation from an RFQ using the Copy Document window, Oracle Purchasing stores the foreign key to your original RFQ in FROM_HEADER_ID. Oracle Purchasing also uses FROM_TYPE_LOOKUP_CODE to indicate that you copied the quotation from an RFQ. Oracle Purchasing does not use SUMMARY_FLAG and ENABLED_FLAG. Because future versions of Oracle Purchasing will use them, SUMMARY_FLAG and ENABLED_FLAG should always be 'N' and 'Y' respectively. You enter document header information in the Header region of the Purchase Orders, RFQs, and Quotations windows.

PO_HEADERS_ARCHIVE_ALL.description: PO_HEADERS_ARCHIVE_ALL contains archived purchase order header information. Oracle Purchasing only archives the following documents: Standard purchase orders, Blanket purchase orders and Contract purchase orders. When you modify a PO_HEADER row, Oracle Purchasing will archive it after you (re)approve the purchase order or after you print it (this depends on the purchasing system options). You can retrieve previous revisions of the purchase order header using this table. The latest archived revision will have LATEST_EXTERNAL_FLAG set to 'Y'. You need one row for each document header you archive. Each row contains the same information as the PO_HEADERS_ALL table. Oracle Purchasing uses this information to ensure you keep copies of your legal documents. Oracle Purchasing does not currently read or retrieve information in this table.

PO_HEADERS_DRAFT_ALL.description: PO_HEADERS_DRAFT_ALL stores draft changes to PO header that have not been accepted by buyer. A record in this table contains the proposed values for the corresponding record in the transaction table PO_HEADERS_ALL. When the draft changes are accepted, the record is moved to PO_HEADERS_ALL. Changes from buyer are automatically accepted. PO_HEADERS_DRAFT_ALL contains all columns from PO_HEADERS_ALL, as well as the following additional columns: DRAFT_ID: primary key of the table; foreign key to PO_DRAFTS; CHANGE_ACCEPTED_FLAG: acceptance status of the draft record; DELETE_FLAG: flag indicating whether the corresponding record in transaction table should be deleted when the draft change is accepted.

PO_INTERFACE_ERRORS.description: Stores the Interface errors from the PDOI Program

PO_LINES_ALL.description: PO_LINES_ALL stores current information about each purchase order line. You need one row for each line you attach to a document. There are five document types that use lines: RFQs, Quotations, Standard purchase orders, Blanket purchase orders and Planned purchase orders. Each row includes the line number, item number, category, unit, price, tax information, matching information, and quantity ordered for the line. Oracle Purchasing uses this information to record and update item and price information for purchase orders, quotations, and RFQs. PO_LINE_ID is the unique system-generated line number invisible to the user. LINE_NUM is the number of the line on the purchase order. Oracle Purchasing uses CONTRACT_ID to reference a contract purchase order from a standard purchase order line.  Oracle Purchasing uses ALLOW_PRICE_OVERRIDE_FLAG, COMMITTED_AMOUNT, QUANTITY_COMMITTED, MIN_RELEASE_AMOUNT only for blanket and planned purchase order lines. The QUANTITY field stores the total quantity of all purchase order shipment lines (found in PO_LINE_LOCATIONS_ALL).

PO_LINES_ARCHIVE_ALL.description: PO_LINES_ARCHIVE_ALL contains archived purchase order line information. Oracle Purchasing only archives lines for the following documents: Standard purchase orders and Blanket purchase orders. When you modify a purchasing line, Oracle Purchasing will archive it after you (re)approve the purchase order or after you print it (this depends on the purchasing system options). You can retrieve revisions of the purchase order line using this table. The latest archived revision will have LATEST_EXTERNAL_FLAG set to 'Y'. You need one row for each document line you archive. Each row contains the same information as the PO_LINES_ALL table. Oracle Purchasing uses this information to ensure you keep copies of your legal documents. Oracle Purchasing does not currently read or retrieve information in this table.

PO_LINES_DRAFT_ALL.description: PO_LINES_DRAFT_ALL stores draft changes to PO lines that have not been accepted by buyer.  A record in this table contains the proposed values for the corresponding record in the transaction table PO_LINES_ALL.  When the draft changes are accepted, the record is moved to PO_LINES_ALL.  Changes from buyer are automatically accepted. PO_LINES_DRAFT_ALL contains all columns from PO_LINES_ALL, as well as the following additional columns: DRAFT_ID:  primary key of the table; foreign key to PO_DRAFTS; CHANGE_ACCEPTED_FLAG:  acceptance status of the draft record; DELETE_FLAG:  flag indicating whether the corresponding record in transaction table should be deleted when the draft change is accepted.

PO_LINES_GT.description: Purchase Order Lines Global Temporary table.

PO_LINES_INTERFACE.description: PO_LINES_INTERFACE is the interface table that holds lines information for the PDOI program to create standard/blanket purchase  orders and catalog quotations. The PDOI program validates your data, derives or defaults additional  information and writes an error message for every validation that  fails into the PO_INTERFACE_ERRORS table. Scope: public Product: PO Display Name: PO Lines Interface Category: BUSINESS_ENTITY PO_BLANKET_PURCHASE_AGREEMENT Category: BUSINESS_ENTITY PO_CONTRACT_PURCHASE_AGREEMENT Category: BUSINESS_ENTITY PO_GLOBAL_BLANKET_AGREEMENT Category: BUSINESS_ENTITY PO_GLOBAL_CONTRACT_AGREEMENT Category: BUSINESS_ENTITY PO_PRICE_BREAKS Category: BUSINESS_ENTITY PO_STANDARD_PURCHASE_ORDER

PO_LINE_LOCATIONS_ALL.description: PO_LINE_LOCATIONS_ALL contains information about purchase order shipment schedules and blanket agreement price breaks. You need one row for each schedule or price break you attach to a document line. There are seven types of documents that use shipment schedules: RFQs, Quotations, Standard purchase orders, Planned purchase orders, Planned purchase order releases, Blanket purchase orders and Blanket purchase order releases. Each row includes the location, quantity, and dates for each shipment schedule. Oracle Purchasing uses this information to record delivery schedule information for purchase orders, and price break information for blanket purchase orders, quotations and RFQs. PO_RELEASE_ID applies only to blanket purchase order release shipments. PO_RELEASE_ID identifies the release on which you placed this shipment. SOURCE_SHIPMENT_ID applies only to planned purchase order release shipments. It identifies the planned purchase order shipment you chose to release from. PRICE_OVERRIDE always equals the purchase order line price for standard purchase order shipments. For blanket and planned purchase orders, PRICE_OVERRIDE depends on the values of the ALLOW_PRICE_OVERRIDE_FLAG and NOT_TO_EXCEED_PRICE in the corresponding row in PO_LINES_ALL: If ALLOW_PRICE_OVERRIDE_FLAG is 'N', then PRICE_OVERRIDE equals UNIT_PRICE in PO_LINES_ALL. If ALLOW_PRICE_OVERRIDE_FLAG is 'Y', the PRICE_OVERRIDE can take any value that is smaller than NOT_TO_EXCEED_PRICE in PO_LINES_ALL. The QUANTITY field corresponds to the total quantity ordered on all purchase order distribution lines (found in PO_DISTRIBUTIONS_ALL). Oracle Purchasing automatically updates QUANTITY_RECEIVED, QUANTITY_ACCEPTED, and QUANTITY_REJECTED when you receive, return, or inspect goods or services. Oracle Payables automatically updates QUANTITY_BILLED when you match an invoice with a purchase order shipment. Oracle Purchasing automatically updates QUANTITY_CANCELLED when you cancel a purchase order shipment. Oracle Purchasing sets APPROVED_FLAG to 'Y' when you approve the corresponding purchase order if there are no problems associated with the shipment and its related distributions. Oracle Purchasing sets ENCUMBERED_FLAG to 'Y' and enters the ENCUMBERED_DATE when you approve a purchase order if you use encumbrance.

PO_LINE_LOCATIONS_ARCHIVE_ALL.description: PO_LINE_LOCATIONS_ARCHIVE_ALL contains archived purchase order shipment information. Oracle Purchasing only archives shipments for the following documents: Standard purchase orders, Planned purchase orders, Planned purchase order releases and Blanket purchase order releases. When you modify a shipment, Oracle Purchasing will archive it after you (re)approve the purchase order or after you print it (this depends on the purchasing system options). You can retrieve revisions of the shipments using this table. The latest archived revision will have LATEST_EXTERNAL_FLAG set to 'Y'. You need one row for each shipment archive. Each row contains the same information as the PO_LINE_LOCATIONS_ALL table. Oracle Purchasing uses this information to ensure you keep copies of your legal documents.

PO_LINE_LOCATIONS_DRAFT_ALL.description: PO_LINE_LOCATIONS_DRAFT_ALL contains draft changes about shipment schedules and blanket agreement price breaks.

PO_LINE_TYPES_B.description: PO_LINE_TYPES_B contains information about the line types you use in your business. You need each row for each line type you use. . Oracle Purchasing uses this information to provide default information when you create a document line using a line type.  Oracle Purchasing also uses this information to control how you enter information on your document lines according to the line type you choose. ORDER_TYPE_LOOKUP_CODE is 'AMOUNT' for an amount-based line type or 'QUANTITY' for a quantity-based line type. . .

PO_LINE_TYPES_TL.description: PO_LINE_TYPES_TL contains translated information for the Purchasing document line types.

PO_MASS_ACTION_RECORDS.description: PO_MASS_ACTION_RECORDS tracks the records to be processed for the mass action requests.

PO_MASS_ACTION_REQUESTS.description: PO_MASS_ACTION_REQUESTS contains information about mass action and update requests for purchasing documents.

PO_NOTIFICATION_CONTROLS.description: PO_NOTIFICATION_CONTROLS contains information about the notification control rules for blanket, planned, and contract purchase orders. There is one record in this table for each notification control rule that an employee creates for a purchase order header. Oracle Alert uses the notification control rules to determine the type of notification to send, the effective date range for the notification, and the document amount that triggers the notification.  . NOTIFICATION_ID is the unique system-generated primary key and is invisible to the user.  . You enter notification control information in the Notification Controls window, accessible through the Purchase Orders window.  .

PO_NOTIFICATION_CTRL_ARCHIVE.description: PO_NOTIFICATION_CTRL_ARCHIVE contains archived information about the notification control rules for blanket, planned, and contract purchase orders. There is one record in this table for each notification control rule that an employee creates for a purchase order header. Oracle Alert uses the notification control rules to determine the type of notification to send, the effective date range for the notification, and the document amount that triggers the notification.  . NOTIFICATION_ID is the unique system-generated primary key and is invisible to the user.  . You enter notification control information in the Notification Controls window, accessible through the Purchase Orders window.  .

PO_NOTIFICATION_CTRL_DRAFT.description: PO_NOTIFICATION_CTRL_DRAFT stores draft changes to PO notification controls that have not been accepted by buyer. A record in this table contains the proposed values for the corresponding record in the transaction table PO_NOTIFICATION_CONTROLS. When the draft changes are accepted, the record is moved to PO_NOTIFICATION_CONTROLS. Changes from buyer are automatically accepted. PO_NOTIFICATION_CTRL_DRAFT contains all columns from PO_NOTIFICATION_CONTROLS, as well as the following additional columns: DRAFT_ID: primary key of the table; foreign key to PO_DRAFTS; CHANGE_ACCEPTED_FLAG: acceptance status of the draft record; DELETE_FLAG: flag indicating whether the corresponding record in transaction table should be deleted when the draft change is accepted.

PO_ONLINE_REPORT_TEXT.description: Contains information about the purchasing documents that fail the approval, cancel or final-close submission checks. There is one record in this table for each error detected during the submission check. Each row includes the error that was detected and the document entity that the error is associated with.

PO_ONLINE_REPORT_TEXT_GT.description: Global Temporary table used for Purchasing Online report Text

PO_PDOI_PROGRESS.description: PO_PDOI_PROGRESS contains the information of the state of Purchasing Document Import Jobs. It is used to make Purchasing Document Import job restartable from the point of failure.

PO_RECENT_ACTIVITIES.description: This table is created to capture the  activities that happen on Purchasing Documents. This table will store historical information about the activities that are relevant to Recent Activities region.

PO_REQ_SPLIT_LINES_GT.description: The Global Temporary table stores the splited requisition line detail.

PO_SESSION_GT.description: The Global Temporary table stores the PO related information to perform submission check validation.

PO_SPCL_HANDLING_TYPES_B.description: PO_SPCL_HANDLING_TYPES_B contains information about the line types you use in your business. You need each row for each line type you use. . Oracle Purchasing uses this information to provide default information when you create a document line using a line type.

PO_SPCL_HANDLING_TYPES_TL.description: PO_SPCL_HANDLING_TYPES_TL contains translated information for Purchasing special handling types.

PO_STAGED_DOCUMENT_HEADERS.description: PO_STAGED_DOCUMENT_HEADERS contains information of staged document used in process requisition.

PO_STAGED_DOCUMENT_LINES.description: PO_STAGED_DOCUMENT_LINES contains information of staged document lines used in process requisition.

PO_SYSTEM_PARAMETERS_ALL.description: PO_SYSTEM_PARAMETERS_ALL stores default, control, and option information you provide to customize Oracle Purchasing to your company's needs. PO_SYSTEM_PARAMETERS_ALL corresponds to the Purchasing Options window. This table has no primary key.  The table should never have more than one row.   . .

PO_UNIQUE_IDENTIFIER_CONT_ALL.description: PO_UNIQUE_IDENTIFIER_CONT_ALL  stores information about the current, highest, system-generated numbers for the Oracle Purchasing tables that require special sequencing.  You need one row for each sequentially system-generated number for each organization. The table includes rows for each of the following: purchase orders, requisitions, receipts, suppliers, quotations, and requests for quotations (RFQs).  . For each organization, there are four rows for each of  the following entities: PO_HEADERS_ALL, PO_REQUISITION_HEADERS_ALL, PO_HEADERS_RFQ and PO_HEADERS_QUOTE.  There are two rows corresponding to the entities PO_VENDORS and RCV_SHIPMENT_HEADERS. . The information for the quotation and RFQ sub-entities is associated with the PO_HEADERS_ALL table entity.  TABLE_NAME values for quotations and RFQs are 'PO_HEADERS_QUOTE' and 'PO_HEADERS_RFQ' respectively. . .

PO_UN_NUMBERS_B.description: PO_UN_NUMBERS_B contains information about the United Nations hazardous materials classification numbers that you use in your business. Oracle Purchasing lets you record UN numbers information to let you comply with Section 19 of the International Safety Rating System. You need one row for each UN number you use.  . Oracle Purchasing uses this information to let you provide UN number information on your purchase orders, requisitions, RFQs, and quotations. . .

PO_UN_NUMBERS_TL.description: PO_UN_NUMBERS_TL contains translated information for the United Nations numbers.

PO_VERSIONS.description: The version data for a purchasing document.

PO_WC_HEADERS.description: PO_WC_HEADERS contains header information for work confirmation object. WORK_CONFIRMATION_ID is the unique system-generated primary key and is invisible to the user. Oracle Purchasing uses PO_HEADER_ID to identify the object against which work confirmation is getting created.

PO_WC_HEADERS_HISTORY.description: PO_WC_HEADERS_HISTORY contains information on corrections performed on work confirmation.

PO_WC_LINES.description: PO_WC_LINES stores information about each work confirmation line. WORK_CONFIRMATION_LINE_ID is the unique system-generated line number invisible to the user.

PO_WC_LINES_HISTORY.description: PO_WC_LINES_HISTORY contains information on the corrections performed on the work confirmation lines.

POR_AGENT_DOCUMENT_REQUESTS.description: POR_AGENT_DOCUMENT_REQUESTS stores and manages information about document requests initiated or processed by agents within the procurement workflow. This table captures metadata such as origins, workflow processing status, related request identifiers, tracking details, error messages, and full audit trail including creation and update information, supporting document traceability and compliance.

POR_AMX_ACTIONS.description: This table contains data for rule actions. Each row contains one action.

POR_AMX_AGGREGATE_ATTRIBUTES.description: POR_AMX_AGGREGATE_ATTRIBUTES stores information about the user defined attributes of summation/aggregation type.

POR_AMX_APPROVAL_GROUPS.description: This table contains the list of approval groups created by the user in BPM worklist. It is used for created approval rule actions. Each row contains the name of one approval group.

POR_AMX_ATTRIBUTES.description: This table stores the approval attributes metadata of a specific approval dimension.  Each dimension can have multiple attributes.

POR_AMX_CONDITIONS.description: This table contains individual conditions for approval rules. Each row contains one condition.

POR_AMX_CONDITION_INS.description: This table contains value data for conditions that use the "is in" operator. Each row contains one value.

POR_AMX_CURRENCY_ATTRIBUTES.description: This table contains information about the user defined attributes of type Currency.

POR_AMX_CUSTOM_ATTRIBUTES.description: This table contains information about the user defined attributes of type custom.

POR_AMX_DIMENSIONS.description: This table stores the approval dimension metadata of a specific approval stage.

POR_AMX_PARTICIPANTS.description: This table stores the approval participants metadata of a specific approval stage.

POR_AMX_RULES.description: This table contains top-level information about approval rules.

POR_AMX_RULESET_CHECKSUMS.description: This table contains information for the checksum for rule set.

POR_AMX_RULES_JOBS.description: This table is used to store the details of the Jobs submitted for deploying rules to BPM.

POR_AMX_RULE_ATTRIBUTE_USAGES.description: This table contains information of attribute usage for rules.

POR_AMX_STAGES.description: The table stores the approval stages metadata of a specific approval task.

POR_AMX_TASKS.description: This table stores the approval task metadata of a document.

POR_APPROVAL_TASK_COMMENTS.description: POR_APPROVAL_TASK_COMMENTS contains comments entered by the approver while approving/rejecting an approval task.

POR_BROWSE_CATEGORIES_B.description: This table stores the non translatable columns of the browsing  categories.

POR_BROWSE_CATEGORIES_TL.description: This table stores the translatable columns of the browsing  categories

POR_BROWSE_NORMALIZED_TREES.description: This table contains the normalized hierarchical structure for browsing category hierarchy stored in POR_BROWSE_TREES.

POR_BROWSE_TREES.description: Table that stores the Browse category tree.

POR_CATALOGS_ALL_B.description: This table stores the Non Translatable columns of the Catalogs.

POR_CATALOGS_ALL_TL.description: This table stores the Translatable columns of the Catalogs.

POR_CATALOG_ASSIGNMENTS.description: This table stores the Id's of the Categories and Agreements assigned to Catalogs.

POR_CATALOG_SEARCH_OBJECTS.description: This is an intermediate table for elastic search index.

POR_CA_GEN_SESSION_GT.description: This global temporary table is used to store the distribution ids and hash code for requisition before calling TAB api.

POR_CONTENT_ZONES_ALL_B.description: This table stores the Non Translatable columns of the Content Zones.

POR_CONTENT_ZONES_ALL_TL.description: This table stores the Translatable columns of the Content Zones

POR_CUR_CONV_FACTOR_SESSION_GT.description: POR_CUR_CONV_FACTOR_SESSION_GT stores the conversion factor which will be used to convert the item price from transactional currency to functional currency.

POR_CZ_ASSIGNMENTS.description: This table stores the Id's of the Catalogs, Smart Forms and Public Lists assigned to Content Zones.

POR_CZ_SECURITY_ASSIGNMENTS.description: This table stores the Id's of the Workers and Requisitioning BU's assigned to Content Zones.

POR_DOC_PARSE_ERRORS.description: The table is used for storing the parse errors which occur during the Agreement Loader file parsing.

POR_DOC_UPLOAD_JOBS.description: This table is used to store the details of the Jobs submitted for uploading lines to an agreement. These details are stored locally and not retrieved from the ESS framework.

POR_INFO_CATALOGS.description: This table stores information related to Informational Catalog.

POR_INFO_TEMPLATES_ALL_B.description: This table holds the non-translated columns for the Information templates Functionality.

POR_INFO_TEMPLATES_ALL_TL.description: This Table will hold the translated values for the Information Template details like name and Description.

POR_INFO_TEMPLATE_USAGES.description: This table is used to store the usages of the Information templates. The Information templates can be used with the Items, Categories or Smart Forms.

POR_ITEMS_CTX_DTLS_TLP.description: Stores the details of the context of items added during the shopping and create requisition process.

POR_ITEMS_CTX_HDRS_TLP.description: This is the table for our CTX items for Search Results.

POR_ITEM_CAT_PARENT_LEVELS.description: This table stores the column flattened representation of purchasing categories stored in POR_BROWSE_TREES table.

POR_LOCAL_CATALOGS.description: This table stores information related to Local Catalogs.

POR_MAP_SET_HEADERS_ALL_B.description: This Table will store the non translatable columns for the Map Sets.

POR_MAP_SET_HEADERS_ALL_TL.description: This table will store the translated columns for the map set details.

POR_MAP_SET_LINES.description: This table will store the actual mapping information for each map set.

POR_ONE_TIME_LOCATIONS.description: This table stored the one time addresses entered by the users when they choose the Deliver to Location type as "One Time"

POR_ONLINE_REPORT_TEXT.description: Contains information of texts of TAB sources for Por TAB diagnostic report.

POR_PL_INFO_TEMPLATE_VALUES.description: Information template values for Procurement Personal Shopping List.

POR_PUBLIC_LIST_HEADERS_ALL_B.description: This Table Stores the Non Translatable columns of the Public Lists.

POR_PUBLIC_LIST_HEADERS_ALL_TL.description: This Table Stores the  Translatable columns of the Public Lists.

POR_PUBLIC_LIST_LINES.description: This table stores the line details for the Public Lists.

POR_PUNCHOUT_CATALOGS.description: This table stores information related to Punchout Catalogs.

POR_PUNCHOUT_ITEMS.description: This table stores information related to punchout items

POR_PUNCHOUT_ITEM_ERRORS.description: This table stores information related to punchout item errors

POR_PURCHASE_REQ_EVENT_HISTORY.description: POR_PURCHASE_REQ_EVENT_HISTORY stores information about payload of purchase request event.  You need one row for each purchase request event has raised. Each row contains the payload information of event raised for purchase request.

POR_PURCHASING_NEWS.description: This table contains Purchasing news for the Requisitioning Business Units.

POR_REQUISITION_HEADERS_ALL.description: POR_REQUISITION_HEADERS_ALL stores information about requisition headers.  You need one row for each requisition header you create. Each row contains the requisition number, preparer, status, and description.  . REQUISITION_HEADER_ID is the unique system-generated requisition number.  REQUISITION_HEADER_ID is invisible to the user. REQUISITION_NUMBER is the number you use to identify the requisition in forms and reports.  Oracle Purchasing generates REQUISITION_NUMBER using the PO_UNIQUE_IDENTIFIER_CONTROL table if you choose to let Oracle Purchasing generate requisition numbers for you.   . POR_REQUISITION_HEADERS_ALL is one of three tables storing requisition information.  POR_REQUISITION_HEADERS_ALL corresponds to the Header region of the Requisitions window.

POR_REQUISITION_LINES_ALL.description: POR_REQUISITION_LINES stores information about requisition lines. You need one row for each requisition line you create.  Each row contains the line number, item number, item category, item description, need-by date, deliver-to location, item quantities, units, prices, requestor, notes, and suggested supplier information for the requisition line.  PARENT_REQ_LINE_ID contains the REQUISITION_LINE_ID from the original requisition line if you exploded or multisourced this requisition line. . POR_REQUISITION_LINES is one of three tables storing requisition information. This table corresponds to the Lines region of the Requisitions window.   . .

POR_REQ_BCEA_DATA.description: This table is used to store budgetary control and encumbrance accounting information.

POR_REQ_BU_OPTIONS_ALL.description: This table stores information related to setup options of Requisitioning BU's.

POR_REQ_CONFIG_COMPS_INT.description: This table stores the base model, sub-model, and options that make up the configuration at the requisition line level.

POR_REQ_DISTRIBUTIONS_ALL.description: POR_REQ_DISTRIBUTIONS_ALL stores information about the accounting distributions associated with each requisition line.  Each requisition line must have at least one accounting distribution. You need one row for each requisition distribution you create. Each row includes the Accounting Flexfield ID and requisition line quantity.  . POR_REQ_DISTRIBUTIONS_ALL is one of three tables storing your requisition information. This table corresponds to the requisition Distributions window, accessible through the Requisitions window. . .

POR_REQ_DISTS_INTERFACE_ALL.description: PO_REQ_DIST_INTERFACE_ALL is the interface table that creates multiple distributions using Requisition Import. Scope: public Product: PO Display Name: PO Requisition Distribution Interface Category: BUSINESS_ENTITY PO_PURCHASE_REQUISITION Category: BUSINESS_ENTITY PO_INTERNAL_REQUISITION

POR_REQ_EVENT_SEQUENCE.description: POR_REQ_EVENT_SEQUENCE stores information about purchase request event sequence.  You need one row for each requisition for which purchase request event has raised. Each row contains the requisition header id, sequence number and event submission flag.

POR_REQ_HEADERS_INTERFACE_ALL.description: PO_REQUISITIONS_INTERFACE_ALL contains requisition information from other applications. Each row includes all the information necessary to create approved or unapproved requisitions in Oracle Purchasing.  The Oracle Purchasing Requisition Import feature uses this information to create new requisition headers, lines and distributions. . You use the Submit Request window to launch the Requisition Import program for any set of rows. You identify the set of rows you want to import by setting the INTERFACE_SOURCE_CODE and BATCH_ID columns appropriately in the interface table. . The Requisition Import program validates your data, derives or defaults additional information and writes an error message for every validation that fails into the PO_INTERFACE_ERRORS table.  Then the program groups and numbers requisition lines according to the criteria you specify in the GROUP_CODE and REQ_NUMBER_SEGMENT1 columns, and creates new requisitions. . Most of the columns in the PO_REQUISITIONS_INTERFACE_ALL table are identical to the corresponding columns in the PO_REQUISITIONS_HEADERS_ALL, PO_REQUISITION_LINES_ALL and PO_REQ_DISTRIBUTIONS_ALL tables. You can additionally enter reference information in the INTERFACE_SOURCE_LINE_ID column. . Scope: public Product: PO Display Name: PO Requisitions Interface Category: BUSINESS_ENTITY PO_PURCHASE_REQUISITION Category: BUSINESS_ENTITY PO_INTERNAL_REQUISITION

POR_REQ_IMPORT_ERRORS.description: POR_REQ_IMPORT_ERRORS stores error information generated in requisition import.

POR_REQ_IMPORT_HDRS_SESSION_GT.description: POR_REQ_IMPORT_HDRS_SESSION_GT stores information of requisition during requisition import. Because of grouping, an interface header can be split into multiple req headers. Hence, two requisition headers from the same interface header can have different values for process_flag. This table is used to store the process flag in the req header level after grouping so that we can report the process flag value for each requisition header in the output VO in the web service case. It also helps in some of our sqls later on to eliminate the need for joining to a subquery.

POR_REQ_INFO_TEMPLATE_VALUES.description: This table stores the requisiton information values as a template.

POR_REQ_LINES_INTERFACE_ALL.description: PO_REQUISITIONS_INTERFACE_ALL contains requisition information from other applications. Each row includes all the information necessary to create approved or unapproved requisitions in Oracle Purchasing.  The Oracle Purchasing Requisition Import feature uses this information to create new requisition headers, lines and distributions. . You use the Submit Request window to launch the Requisition Import program for any set of rows. You identify the set of rows you want to import by setting the INTERFACE_SOURCE_CODE and BATCH_ID columns appropriately in the interface table. . The Requisition Import program validates your data, derives or defaults additional information and writes an error message for every validation that fails into the PO_INTERFACE_ERRORS table.  Then the program groups and numbers requisition lines according to the criteria you specify in the GROUP_CODE and REQ_NUMBER_SEGMENT1 columns, and creates new requisitions. . Most of the columns in the PO_REQUISITIONS_INTERFACE_ALL table are identical to the corresponding columns in the PO_REQUISITIONS_HEADERS_ALL, PO_REQUISITION_LINES_ALL and PO_REQ_DISTRIBUTIONS_ALL tables. You can additionally enter reference information in the INTERFACE_SOURCE_LINE_ID column. . Scope: public Product: PO Display Name: PO Requisitions Interface Category: BUSINESS_ENTITY PO_PURCHASE_REQUISITION Category: BUSINESS_ENTITY PO_INTERNAL_REQUISITION

POR_REQ_SPLIT_LINES_GT.description: Global Temporary tableGlobal Temporary tableGlobal Temporary tableGlobal Temporary tableGlobal Temporary tableGlobal Temporary table

POR_SEARCH_INDEX_OPTIONS.description: This table is used to store the details about index options for POR_ITEMSCTXDESC_HDRS index.

POR_SEARCH_PHRASES.description: This table stores information of the search phrases which did not return any results and its frequency.

POR_SEARCH_PHRASES_LOG.description: This table stores information of search phrases which did not return any results and its corresponding search date.

POR_SESSION_GT.description: POR_SESSION_GT is a Global temporary table that stores the session data.

POR_SHORT_URL_DETAILS.description: This table contains information about the short URL.

POR_SMART_FORMS_ALL_B.description: This Table Stores the Non Translatable columns of the Smart Forms.

POR_SMART_FORMS_ALL_TL.description: This table is used to store the Translated Columns for the Smart Forms information.

POR_SMART_FORM_CAT_ASSIGNMENTS.description: This table stores the category assignments to the Smart Form for catalog browsing.

POR_UPGRADE_PROCESSES_HISTORY.description: This table is used to store the details of the Jobs submitted while upgrading.

POR_USER_PREF_ACCOUNTS_ALL.description: Accounting user preference for requisition creation.

PON_ACCEPTANCES.description: PON_ACCEPTANCES stores auction and RFQ award information by response (quote/bid) item line. Each record in this table represents either an award or rejection decision indicated by the ACCEPTANCE_TYPE column.

PON_ACKNOWLEDGEMENTS.description: PON_ACKNOWLEDGEMENTS table stores information regarding the supplier user's acknowledgment of  negotiation amendments.

PON_ACTION_HISTORY.description: PON_ACTION_HISTORY stores the information regarding to the action taken by the buyers on a specific negotiation, such as cancel, approve and reject.

PON_APPROVAL_USER_COMMENTS.description: PON_APPROVAL_USER_COMMENTS table stores the comment entered by the approver while taking the approval action. User can enter multiple comments for a given approval cycle.

PON_ATTACHMENT_SUMMARY.description: Stores the summarized information of individual attachments.

PON_ATTRIBUTES_INTERFACE.description:
PON_ATTRIBUTES_INTERFACE is used during
                                         BPA renegotiation. This table stores
                                         base and catalog descriptors in
                                         addition to the sourcing attributes.
                                         IP_CATEGORY_ID is 0 for base
                                         descriptors.

PON_ATTRIBUTE_LISTS.description: PON_ATTRIBUTE_LISTS stores the name of company-wide negotiation attribute lists for repeated use.  LIST_STATUS column indicates whether the list is active.

PON_ATTRIBUTE_SCORES.description: PON_ATTRIBUTE_SCORES stores the scoring criteria for a particular attribute in either an auction item or a reusable attribute list.  The master table is PON_AUCTION_ATTRIBUTES.

PON_AUCTION_ATTRIBUTES.description: PON_AUCTION_ATTRIBUTES stores item-specific attributes defined by the negotiation owner for auctions, RFQ, and RFIs. These attributes enable buyers to request information with more detail than is possible with the standard fields. COPIED_FROM_CAT_FLAG indicates whether the attributes was copied from the category definition in the catalog. If ATTRIBUTE_LIST_ID is populated, then the attribute belongs to a reusable attributes list. Otherwise, if AUCTION_HEADER_ID is populated, then the attribute belongs to a negotiation or a negotiation template. Only one of these two columns is populated for any single record.

PON_AUCTION_CURRENCY_RATES.description:
PON_AUCTION_CURRENCY_RATES stores
                                         transaction-specific currency rates for
                                         multi currency auctions, RFQs, and RFIs
                                         if the negotiation owner defines the
                                         currency rates. If the negotiation uses
                                         company-wide currency rates, then no
                                         records are created in this table.

PON_AUCTION_CURRENCY_RATES_.description: PON_AUCTION_CURRENCY_RATES stores transaction-specific currency rates for multi currency auctions, RFQs, and RFIs if the negotiation owner defines the currency rates. If the negotiation uses company-wide currency rates, then no records are created in this table.

PON_AUCTION_HEADERS_ALL.description: PON_AUCTION_HEADERS stores auctions, RFQs, and RFIs header information.  Each row includes general negotiation information such as type, style, business terms and conditions, response controls, et cetera. The DOCTYPE_ID column indicates whether the transaction is an auction, RFQ, or RFI.

PON_AUCTION_HEADERS_ALL_.description: PON_AUCTION_HEADERS stores auctions, RFQs, and RFIs header information.  Each row includes general negotiation information such as type, style, business terms and conditions, response controls, et cetera. The DOCTYPE_ID column indicates whether the transaction is an auction, RFQ, or RFI.

PON_AUCTION_ITEM_PRICES_ALL.description: PON_AUCTION_ITEM_PRICES stores negotiations item information.  For every auction, RFQ, and RFI line there is a record in this table.

PON_AUCTION_ITEM_PRICES_ALL_.description: PON_AUCTION_ITEM_PRICES stores negotiations item information.  For every auction, RFQ, and RFI line there is a record in this table.

PON_AUCTION_SECTIONS.description: PON_AUCTION_SECTIONS table stores sections for Requirements (Header Attributes) and attribute groups for Line Level Attributes.

PON_AUCTION_SHIPMENTS_ALL.description:
PON_AUCTION_SHIPMENTS_ALL stores
                                         negotiations shipment information (for
                                         now, this is limited to price breaks).

PON_AUCTION_SUMMARY.description: PON_AUCTION_SUMMARY stores temporary data to be displayed in the summary page before a negotiation is created.

PON_AUC_ATTRIBUTES_INTERFACE.description: PON_AUC_ATTRIBUTES_INTERFACE temporarily stores negotiation attribute information that the negotiation owner is uploading from a spreadsheet.  Once the information is uploaded,  the interface is processed and the errors are reported in PON_INTERFACE_ERRORS.

PON_AUC_ATTR_SCORES_INTERFACE.description: PON_AUC_ATTR_SCORES_INTERFACE temporarily stores negotiation requirement acceptable values information that the negotiation owner is uploading from a spreadsheet.  Once the information is uploaded,  the interface is processed and the errors are reported in PON_INTERFACE_ERRORS.

PON_AUC_DOCTYPES_B.description: PON_AUC_DOCTYPE table stores information about document types (Auction, RFQ and RFI).

PON_AUC_DOCTYPES_TL.description: PON_AUC_DOCTYPE_TL table is used to support multiple languages. It stores the document name of each document type in different languages.

PON_AUC_HEADERS_INTERFACE.description: PON_AUC_HEADERS_INTERFACE holds Negotiation header information that will be used to create a draft sourcing negotiation.

PON_AUC_ITEMS_INTERFACE.description: PON_AUC_ITEMS_INTERFACE holds Negotiation item  information that will be used to create a draft sourcing negotiation.

PON_AUC_PRICE_ELEMENTS_INT.description:
PON_AUC_PRICE_ELEMENTS_INT temporarily
                                         stores negotiation price element
                                         information that the negotiation owner
                                         is uploading from a spreadsheet. Once
                                         the information is uploaded the
                                         interface is processed and the errors
                                         are reported in PON_INTERFACE_ERRORS.

PON_AUC_PRICE_TIERS_INT.description:
PON_AUC_PRICE_TIERS_INT temporarily stores negotiation quantity
based price tiers or price breaks information that the negotiation owner is
uploading from a spreadsheet. Once the information is uploaded, the interface
is processed and the errors are reported in PON_INTERFACE_ERRORS.

PON_AUC_SHIPMENTS_INTERFACE.description:
PON_AUC_SHIPMENTS_INTERFACE
                                         storesNegotiation shipments (for now,
                                         limited to price breaks) information
                                         that will be used to create a draft
                                         sourcing negotiation.

PON_AWARD_ALLOCATIONS.description: PON_AWARD_ALLOCATIONS contains the allocation of each awarded response to the backing requisitions for a particular negotiation line. This table will have rows only if the negotiation line originated from a backing requisition.

PON_AWARD_ALLOCATIONS_HISTORY.description: PON_AWARD_ALLOCATIONS_HISTORY stores the back up of records in PON_AWARD_ALLOCATIONS when award is reopened.

PON_AWARD_ITEMS_INTERFACE.description:
PON_AWARD_ITEMS_INTERFACE temporarily
                                         stores auction and RFQ award
                                         information that it is uploaded from a
                                         spreadsheet. Once the information is
                                         uploaded, the interface is processed
                                         and the errors are reported in
                                         PON_INTERFACE_ERRORS

PON_BACKING_REQUISITIONS.description:
PON_BACKING_REQUISITIONS stores the
                                         requisition_line_id (s) and certain
                                         other requisition line information for
                                         all the requisitions backing a
                                         negotiation item line.

PON_BACKING_REQ_HISTORY.description: PON_BACKING_REQ_HISTORY stores the back up of records in PON_BACKING_REQUISITIONS when award is reopened.

PON_BIDDING_PARTIES.description: PON_BIDDING_PARTIES stores the trading partners that are either part of a trading partner list or were invited to participate in a negotiation.     If AUCTION_HEADER_ID is populated, then the trading partner was invited to a participate in a negotiation.  Otherwise, if LIST_ID is populated, then the trading partner is a member of a trading partners list.  Only one of these two columns is populated for any single record.

PON_BIDDING_PARTIES_.description: PON_BIDDING_PARTIES stores the trading partners that are either part of a trading partner list or were invited to participate in a negotiation.     If AUCTION_HEADER_ID is populated, then the trading partner was invited to a participate in a negotiation.  Otherwise, if LIST_ID is populated, then the trading partner is a member of a trading partners list.  Only one of these two columns is populated for any single record.

PON_BIDDING_PARTY_CONTACTS.description: PON_BIDDING_PARTY_CONTACTS stores the trading partner contacts of the invited suppliers of PON_BIDDING_PARTIES. This table is used to capture all the contacts that need to receive notifications.

PON_BIDDING_TERMS_B.description: PON_BIDDING_TERMS stores contractual information such as business terms that a company requires its bidders and suppliers to accept before they can participate in a negotiation.  Terms are version controlled which means that a new version is created every time the term is changed in the default language. ENABLED_FLAG column indicates whether the term is active.  Note that a term is enabled at the term level, not per language.

PON_BIDDING_TERMS_TL.description: PON_BIDDING_TERMS_TL provides multi-lingual support (MLS) for terms definitions.  MLS enables you to store data defined in multiple languages.  The VERSION_NUMBER column is a reference to the terms version number in PON_BIDDING_TERMS

PON_BID_ATTRIBUTE_VALUES.description: PON_BID_ATTRIBUTE_VALUES stores trading partners' responses to item attributes defined by the negotiation owner.

PON_BID_ATTR_VALUES_INTERFACE.description: PON_BID_ATTR_VALUES_INTERFACE temporarily stores responses to item attributes that uploaded from a spreadsheet.  Once the information is uploaded, the interface is processed and the errors are reported in  PON_INTERFACE_ERRORS

PON_BID_BUS_CLASSIFICATION_INT.description: PON_BID_BUS_CLASS_INTERFACE temporarily stores responses to business classifications  that are uploaded from a spreadsheet.  Once the information is uploaded, the interface is processed and the errors are reported in  PON_INTERFACE_ERRORS

PON_BID_HEADERS.description: PON_BID_HEADERS stores responses to negotiations.  This tables stores header level information, such as response number, trading partner and contact information, business terms and conditions, currency, et cetera.    Oracle Sourcing creates a record in this table for every response to a negotiation.  The STATUS column indicates whether the response is:    -  the most recent available in the marketplace (ACTIVE)    - a disqualified response (DISQUALIFIED)   - an old response (ARCHIVED)

PON_BID_HEADERS_INTERFACE.description: PON_BID_HEADERS_INTERFACE is the interface table for bid headers for xml spreadsheet upload during bidding.

PON_BID_ITEM_PRICES.description: Table PON_BID_ITEM_PRICES stores the line level information such as price, quantity etc for a response submitted by a supplier on a negotiation.

PON_BID_ITEM_PRICES_INTERFACE.description: PON_BID_ITEMS_PROCES_INTERFACE temporarily stores response information that it is uploaded from a spreadsheet.  Once the information is uploaded, the interface is processed and the errors are reported in PON_INTERFACE_ERRORS

PON_BID_PO_LINES.description: PON_BID_PO_LINES stores the ines corresponding to the PO in PON_BID_PO_NUMBERS

PON_BID_PO_LINES_HISTORY.description: PON_BID_PO_LINES_HISTORY stores the back up of records in PON_BID_PO_LINES when award is reopened.

PON_BID_PO_NUMBERS.description: PON_BID_PO_NUMBERS stores the reference of the various PO/BPA created form a response. For a negotiation with outcome as blanket/contract purchase agreement there will be one record in this table per response. For a negotiation with Standard Purchase Order outcome this table can have multiple records per response. This can happen if the awarded response has lines created for different requisition business units or the corresponding negotiation line is created from requisitions with same requisition business unit but different tax attributes.

PON_BID_PO_NUMBERS_HISTORY.description: PON_BID_PO_NUMBERS_HISTORY stores the back up of records in PON_BID_PO_NUMBERS when award is reopened.

PON_BID_PO_ORG_ASSIGNMENTS.description: PON_BID_PO_ORG_ASSIGNMENTS will store the org assignments for the Agreements being created from sourcing.

PON_BID_PRICE_ELEMENTS.description: PON_BID_PRICE_ELEMENTS stores details of price element bids in an auction or RFQ. Each row includes specific information about the price element bids such as auction or RFQ header ID, bid number, price element type ID, auction currency value, bid currency value etc.

PON_BID_PRICE_ELEMENTS_INT.description: PON_BID_PRICE_ELEMENTS_INT is the interface table for price element for spreadsheet upload during bidding.

PON_BID_REQTS_INT.description: Temporarily stores comments to REQUIREMENTS uploaded from a spreadsheet.

PON_BID_REQT_VALUES_INT.description: Temporarily stores responses to REQUIREMENTS uploaded from a spreadsheet.

PON_BID_REQUIREMENTS.description: PON_BID_REQUIREMENTS stores the response details such as response comment for each requirement on the section. The actual response value to a requirement is stored in PON_BID _REQUIREMENT_VALUES.

PON_BID_REQUIREMENT_VALUES.description: PON_BID_REQUIREMENT_VALUES stores the response values for requirements, which could be a date, text or number value, to a given requirement on a negotiation. For question of type multiple choice multiple selection, an entry is created in this table for each of the multiple choice selections from the supplier or internal responder.

PON_BID_SECTIONS.description: PON_BID_SECTIONS stores various sections included in a response along with details such as if a section is visited.

PON_BID_SHIPMENTS.description: PON_BID_SHIPMENTS stores responses' shipment  information (for now, limited to price breaks).

PON_BID_SHIPMENTS_INT.description: PON_BID_SHIPMENTS_INT is the interface table for price breaks for spreadsheet upload during bidding.

PON_BUYER_ACTIVITIES.description: This table is created to capture the buyer activities as they occur. This table will store historical information about the activities that are relevant to Recent Activities region.

PON_DOCTYPE_STYLES_B.description: PON_DOCTYPE_STYLES stores whether a style is enabled for a document type.

PON_DOCTYPE_STYLES_TL.description: This table stores the negotiation display name, response display name and the default content for cover page, introductionn, terms, requirements and lines.

PON_FBDI_ERRORS_INT.description: This table stores the errors from the FBDI process while importing negotiation lines and suppliers.

PON_INTERFACE_ERRORS.description: PON_INTERFACE_ERRORS stores the error information resulting from processing the different negotiation interfaces (item, response, and award upload).  The interface is identified in INTERFACE_TYPE and BATCH_ID indicates the interface job that produced the error.

PON_IOT_CONFIGURATION.description: PON_IOT_CONFIGURATION stores various IOT configuration values that is being used on the Supplier Recommendation feature.

PON_IOT_HEADER_SUPP_RECOMM.description: Supplier Recommendations are stored in this table at Header level.

PON_IOT_LEARNING_RUNS.description: The scheduler program runs are logged in this table to track the movement of data between sourcing cloud and the IoT analytics. This would also record the learning triggers that were initiated by the sourcing cloud.

PON_IOT_LINE_SUPP_RECOMM.description: Supplier Recommendations are stored in this table at LINE level.

PON_IOT_SCORING_REQ_TRAIL.description: PON_IOT_SCORING_REQ_TRAIL records each call to IOT Scoring API to fetch recommendations. Stores REQUEST PAYLOAD sent to the API and RESPONSE PAYLOAD received from the API.

PON_IOT_SUPP_TRANS_EXT.description: PON_IOT_SUPP_TRANS_EXT stores REQUEST_FOR_INFORMATION, REQUEST_FOR_QUOTE, BUYER_AUCTION, STANDARD and BLANKET transactions. Each record in this table represents past negotiation and purchasing transactions.

PON_ITEM_PRICES_INTERFACE.description: PON_ITEM_PRICES_INTERFACE temporarily stores negotiation item information that the negotiation owner is uploading from a spreadsheet.  Once the information is uploaded the interface is processed and the errors are reported in PON_INTERFACE_ERRORS It is used by auctions and RFQs

PON_JOB_MONITORING.description: The job run table stores information about each execution of a scheduled job.

PON_LINES_INT.description: This interface table holds negotiation line information which is loaded by the user for import. The FBDI process uses this information to create, update or delete negotiation lines. The process also validates this data, derives or defaults additional information and writes an error message for every validation that fails into the PON_FBDI_INT_ERRORS table.

PON_LINE_COST_FACTORS_INT.description: This interface table holds negotiation line external cost factors information which is loaded by the user for import. The FBDI process uses this information to create, update or delete line external cost factors. The process also validates this data, derives or defaults additional information and writes an error message for every validation that fails into the PON_FBDI_INT_ERRORS table.

PON_LINE_ICF_SUMMARY.description: This table stores the summarized total of 3 pricing basis for a given supplier and negotiation line.

PON_LINE_PRICE_TIERS_INT.description:
PON_LINE_PRICE_TIERS_INT is the interface table that holds line shipment or
price tier information for the FBDI program to create negotiations. The
FBDI program validates the data, derives or defaults additional information and
writes an error message for every validation that fails into the
PON_FBDI_INT_ERRORS table.

PON_LIST_PRICE_ELEMENTS.description: PON_LIST_PRICE_ELEMENTS stores details of cost factors in a reusable cost factor list. Each row includes specific information about the cost factors in the list.

PON_NEGOTIATION_ABSTRACTS.description: PON_NEGOTIATION_ABSTRACTS table stores information related to negotiation abstracts.

PON_NEGOTIATION_STYLES_B.description: PON_NEGOTIATION_STYLES stores  negotiation style related information.

PON_NEGOTIATION_STYLES_TL.description: PON_NEGOTIATION_STYLES_TL stores language specific negotiation style information.

PON_NEG_ABSTRACT_CONFIG.description: PON_NEG_ABSTRACT_CONFIG stores Abstract configuration values that external users view on Abstract listing page, such as company name, company logo, language, time zone, date format, time format and number format. Buyer could also specify the header/footer content to be shown on Abstract.

PON_NEG_AUDIT_ACTIVITIES.description: This table is created to capture the activities related to negotiation life cycle as they occur for buyer user.

PON_NEG_TEAM_MEMBERS.description: PON_NEG_TEAM_MEMBERS stores details of Collaboration Team members in a negotiation or template. Each row includes specific information about the Collaboration Team members such as auction header id, template id, user name, menu name and collaboration related information.

PON_NEG_TEAM_MEMBERS_.description: PON_NEG_TEAM_MEMBERS stores details of Collaboration Team members in a negotiation or template. Each row includes specific information about the Collaboration Team members such as auction header id, template id, user name, menu name and collaboration related information.

PON_NEG_TERMS_ACCEPTANCE.description: PON_NEG_TERMS_ACCEPTANCE table stores the acceptance of terms by the supplier contacts for a given negotiation. A row is created for each supplier contact who has either accepted/rejected the terms for the negotiation.

PON_NOTIFICATION_SUBSCRIPTION.description: PON_NOTIFICATION_SUBSCRIPTION table stores information about notification subscriptions for document types (Auction, RFQ and RFI).

PON_OBJECTIVE_NEGOTIATIONS.description: PON_OBJECTIVE_NEGOTIATIONS stores the mapping of negotiations to objectives.

PON_PARTY_LINE_EXCLUSIONS.description: PON_PARTY_LINE_EXCLUSIONS stores negotiation line restrictions for a particular supplier/line combination

PON_PRICE_ELEMENTS.description: PON_PRICE_ELEMENTS stores details of price elements in an auction or RFQ. Each row includes specific information about the price elements such as auction or RFQ header ID, line number, list ID, pricing basis, value etc.

PON_PRICE_ELEMENT_LISTS.description: PON_PRICE_ELEMENT_LISTS stores details of price element lists.Each row includes specific information about each price element list such as list ID, list status, trading partner ID, name, description etc.

PON_PRICE_ELEMENT_TYPES_B.description: PON_PRICE_ELEMENT_TYPES stores details of cost factors.    Cost factors include costs associated with the movement of goods from one location to another.  They also include intangible costs such as the cost of switching over to a new supplier when used in the Sourcing process.  This component was called "Price Elements" or "Price Factors" in earlier releases.

PON_PRICE_ELEMENT_TYPES_TL.description: PON_PRICE_ELEMENT_TYPES_TL stores details of price element type languages. Each row includes specific information about each price element language such as language, source language, price element type ID, trading partner ID, name, description etc.

PON_PROGRAM_HEADERS.description: PON_PROGRAM_HEADERS stores the header information of the programs. Each row includes general program information like title, owner, visibility etc.

PON_PROGRAM_OBJECTIVES.description: PON_PROGRAM_OBJECTIVES stores the objectives associated with the program.

PON_PROGRAM_TEAM_MEMBERS.description: PON_PROGRAM_TEAM_MEMBERS stores various stakeholders and interested parties who need to see a sourcing program.

PON_REQUIREMENTS.description: PON_REQUIREMENTS stores header level requirements defined by the negotiation owner for auctions, RFQ, and RFIs. These requirements enable buyers to request information with more detail than is possible with the standard fields.

PON_REQUIREMENTS_.description: PON_REQUIREMENTS stores header level requirements defined by the negotiation owner for auctions, RFQ, and RFIs. These requirements enable buyers to request information with more detail than is possible with the standard fields.

PON_REQUIREMENTS_INT.description: PON_REQUIREMENTS_INT temporarily stores negotiation requirement information that the negotiation owner is uploading from a spreadsheet.

PON_REQUIREMENT_SCORES.description: PON_REQUIREMENT_SCORES stores the scoring criteria for a header level requirement.

PON_REQUIREMENT_SCORES_INT.description: PON_REQUIREMENT_SCORES_INT table temporarily stores the scoring criteria for a requirement that the negotiation owner is uploading from a spreadsheet.

PON_REQUIREMENT_SECTIONS.description: PON_REQUIREMENT_SECTIONS table stores section information for Requirements (Header Attributes).

PON_REQUIREMENT_SECTIONS_.description: PON_REQUIREMENT_SECTIONS table stores section information for Requirements (Header Attributes).

PON_REQUIREMENT_SECTIONS_INT.description: PON_REQUIREMENT_SECTIONS_INT table temporarily stores section information for requirements that the negotiation owner is uploading from a spreadsheet.

PON_SCORING_TEAMS.description: This table stores information about scoring teams that may be created optionally for a negotiation. A scoring team is a group of collaboration team members who are assigned the responsibility of evaluating various negotiation sections.

PON_SCORING_TEAM_MEMBERS.description: PON_SCORING_TEAM_MEMBERS is a logical intersection entity between PON_NEG_TEAM_MEMBERS and PON_SCORING_TEAMS. It lists the members for each scoring team for a given negotiation. Typically this will be a subset of those collaboration team members.

PON_SEARCH_SUGGESTIONS_TLP.description: This table stores search suggestion values which are used for auto suggest feature.

PON_SELLER_AUCTION_BIDDERS.description: Table to store bidder's data for seller auction

PON_SELLER_AUCTION_HEADERS.description: Table for seller auction's header level details

PON_SELLER_AUCTION_LINES.description: Table to store line level details for seller auctions.

PON_SELLER_AUC_BID_HEADERS.description: This table stores header level data for Seller Auction's responses.

PON_SELLER_AUC_BID_LINES.description: This table stores line level data for Seller Auction's responses.

PON_SFO_PARAMETERS_GT.description: Table PON_SFO_PARAMETERS_GT is a temporary table which is being used to hold Sourcing PO data to generate SFO parameters in Draft Purchase Order Creation Flow

PON_SUPPLIER_ACCESS.description: PON_SUPPLIER_ACCESS will record the lock status for a supplier.

PON_SUPPLIER_ACTIVITIES.description: PON_SUPPLIER_ACTIVITIES will contain a row for each distinct activity performed by a Supplier User on the Negotiation as he/she navigates through the bidding flow.

PON_SUPPLIER_ACTIVITIES_HEADER.description: PON_SUPPLIER_ACTIVITIES_HEADER will contain a row for each distinct activity performed by a Supplier User on the Negotiation as he/she navigates  through the bidding  flow.

PON_SUPPLIER_ICF_VALUES.description: This table stores the internal cost factor values which buyer applies to supplier/site combination.

PON_TEAM_BID_SCORES.description: PON_TEAM_BID_SCORES table stores the scoring information provided by a team member at the response level.

PON_TEAM_REQUIREMENT_SCORES.description: PON_TEAM_REQUIREMENT_SCORES table stores the score details entered by a team member for each manually scored requirement response.

PON_TEMP_LOBS.description: Temporary table that stores chunked clobs created while genrating response csv of a negotiation. This table also stores translated strings details as clob_data for which chunk_id value will be -1

PON_THREADS.description: PON_THREADS table stores the thread level details initiated through the online discussion feature. There can be multiple threads initiated for a single negotiation. A thread can be initiated by a buyer or a supplier

PON_THREAD_MESSAGES.description: PON_THREAD_MESSAGES table stores the message level details within a thread initiated through the online discussion feature. This table stores the subject and content of the message and the message sender details. There can be multiple messages associated to a thread. This table also stores the type of broadcast for a message.

PON_THREAD_MESSAGE_RECIPIENTS.description: PON_THREAD_MESSAGE_RECIPIENTS table stores the recipients for a message initiated through the online discussion feature. Each row in this table corresponds to an individual recipient for a message. The table also maintains the message status (Unread/Read/Replied) in context of a recipient for a message.

POI_APPROVED_RESULTS.description: This table contains information about the classification results. After a classification batch is approved, results of the classification are written to this table instead of the source table.

POI_ASSISTED_RESULTS.description: This table contains information about the category suggestions for each transaction. When the user manually updates a category using the assisted mode, two category suggestions are provided to help them classify the transaction.

POI_BATCHES.description: This table contains information about the classification batches. A batch is created when the classification process is run for a data set. The information available about the batch is: batch name, knowledge base used, taxonomy name, and batch approval status. There is one record for each batch in the table.

POI_CLASSIFICATION_CONTROLS.description: This table contains information about classification controls. Classification controls alter the default  behavior of some of the areas of Oracle Fusion Spend Classification. Values for each classification control is shipped with the product; however, users can change them based on their requirements.

POI_CLSF_BATCHES_B.description: This table contains information about the classification batches. A batch is created when the classification process is run for a data set. The information available about the batch is: batch name, knowledge base used, taxonomy name, and batch approval status. There is one record for each batch in the table.

POI_CLSF_BATCHES_TL.description: This table contains information about the classification batches. A batch is created when the classification process is run for a data set. The information available about the batch is: batch name, knowledge base used, taxonomy name, and batch approval status. There is one record for each batch in the table.

POI_DATASET_ATTRIBUTES_MAP.description: This table stores information about the additional data fields that can be used for improving spend classification results.

POI_DATASET_DEFINITIONS.description: This table stores data set view build definitions using seeded and mapped spend attributes to create classification data set view.

POI_DATASET_EXT_DEFINITIONS.description: This table is used to store  user defined data set view definitions that is build using spend attributes to create classification data set view.

POI_DATASET_HEADERS.description: Table stores the information of dataset purpose and last used date details.

POI_DATASET_SOURCE_TABLES.description: This table stores dataset additional attributes lookup values as source table & its join conditions which can be used for improving spend classification results.

POI_DEBUG_LOG.description: This table contains the debug log. Each row contains one debug message.

POI_H_MODELS.description: This table contains information about the knowledge base. Knowledge base is an artificial intelligence or machine learning model that is generated using the training set data and taxonomy. Knowledge base is used by the user to classify their spend data that is unclassified or misclassified or is required to be classified using a different taxonomy.

POI_H_MODELS_HISTORY.description: This table contains information about the actions taken on a knowledge base. It is recommended to improve the knowledge base with new training set data regularly. The details of these improvements are stored in this table.

POI_KB_DATASETS.description: This table contains information about the data sets that are used to create a knowledge base. One or more data sets with purpose training can be used to generate the knowledge base.

POI_KB_EXCLUDED_WORDS.description: Table that contains the list of words that are excluded from the classification process. It contains the excluded words against each knowledge base.

POI_KEYWORDS.description: This table contains the keyword statistics for every transaction record on which the classification process was run. For each transaction, details about all the available keywords in the transaction, and the weightage assigned to each keyword by the artificial intelligence or machine learning engine is stored. This type of keyword analysis is performed during the classification process and stored in this table. Users can see this keyword analysis in the transaction details page.

POI_KEYWORDS_MAP.description: This table stores information about the keywords that are used for analyzing spend classification knowledge base.

POI_MODEL_ATTR_STATS.description: This table stores information about the attribute level metrics data fields that can be used for analyzing spend classification knowledge base.

POI_MODEL_METRICS.description: This table stores information about the advance metrics data fields that can be used for analyzing spend classification knowledge base.

POI_MODEL_METRICS_DATA.description: This table stores data fields that can be used to improve spend classification knowledge base.

POI_MODEL_STATISTICS.description: This table contains key statistics about the knowledge base creation process. Statistics like how many training set records were used for each unique category and knowledge base combination is available in this table. This table also includes the training confidence for each unique category and knowledge base combination.

POI_OP_PROGRESS.description: This table contains information about the knowledge base creation and classification process. Detailed information about the progress of these processes is available in this table.

POI_PHRASES_MAP.description: This table stores phrase attributes lookup values as additional data fields which are used to normalize the keyword values from phrases after the classification process.

POI_PROFILE_OPTIONS.description: These are classification controls. They are not same as FND Profile Options.

POI_REQ_CAT_RECOM_TRAIN.description: This table contains information about the training data to train requisition category recommendation model. This model is used to recommend category for a requisition line when user provides only requisition line information and not category. This data is extracted from core purchasing tables using scheduled extraction process.

POI_RESET_ACTIVITY_STATUS.description: This table contains information about the reset action on classified data sets. Reset action sets the data set back to its original state, the state before running classification process on the data set. After reset action, users can submit the data set for classification again.

POI_RESULTS.description: This table contains information about the classification results. The best and second best category suggestion for each and every level of a category in a transaction is stored in this table. It also contains the confidence percentage for both the suggestions.

POI_RULES.description: This table contains information about the rules that are run during the classification process. Rules can be run before (rule type is preprocessing) or after (rule type is postprocessing) the artificial intelligence or machine learning engine starts suggesting categories for each transaction.

POI_RULES_B.description: This table contains information about the rules that are run during the classification process. Rules can be run before (rule type is preprocessing) or after (rule type is postprocessing) the artificial intelligence or machine learning engine starts suggesting categories for each transaction.

POI_RULE_CONDITIONS.description: This table contains information about all the rule conditions based on which the rules will be used before or after the classification process. Conditions are qualifiers, which generate a subset of the data set for which the rule actions are applicable.

POI_RULE_RUN_HIST.description: This table contains information about the rules and their run history. Rules are used to change the classification results. They can also be used to normalize the data before or after the classification process.

POI_SA_MODEL.description: This table contains the details of the internal models which are used by the datamining process.

POI_SA_MODEL_OP.description: This table contains the details of  internal model operations when creating knowledge base.

POI_SA_OP_JOBS.description: This table contains the details of the internal model job used when creating a new knowledge base.

POI_SA_OP_PROGRESS.description: This table contains information about the internal model creation progress tracking when creating a new knowledge base.

POI_SA_SUPER_MODEL.description: This table contains the details of the internal super model used when creating a new knowledge base.

POI_SM_OPERATION_METRICS.description: This table stores the details of internal model operation metrics.

POI_SM_TABLE_COLUMNS.description: This table stores the details of internal model table columns for knowledge base creation.

POI_SM_TABLE_OBJECTS.description: This table stores the details of the objects created and required for knowledge base creation.

POI_SOURCE_TAXONOMY.description: This table contains information about the source taxonomy setup data. For example, for each source taxonomy, it contains the record of category information like category ID, category name, and category description.

POI_SPEND_DATA.description: This table contains information about the transactions within a data set. This is the raw data that needs to be classified by using one or more knowledge bases. The data in this table can also be used to create a training set or a sample training set.
